title: Cell culture
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4rd8a","text":"This protocol explains the cell culture and characterization of lines ND1014, N1, and ND27760 from Synthetic mRNAs Drive Highly Efficient iPS Cell Differentiation to Dopaminergic Neurons. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":99,"length":87}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4qhlp","text":"Obtain approval to work with human stem cells from an appropriate Institutional Review Board. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"d6eg5","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for safety and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Obtain human iPSC lines from Coriell Cell Repositories or through reprogramming patient cells.
Characterize pluripotency of iPSCs by immunochemistry for pluripotent cell markers (NANOG, OCT4, TRA‐1‐60, and SSEA‐3) and through embryoid body formation assay.
Maintain iPSCs as feeder‐free cultures in mTESR1 medium in 5% CO 2 /95% air condition at 37 °C and passage using ReLeSR.
Perform karyotype analysis of G‐banded metaphase chromosomes to confirm the chromosomal integrity of iPSCs.
